title: `podman volume prune`: match Docker defaults, add `--all` flag
body:
The `podman volume prune` now matches *Docker behavior* by pruning only anonymous unused volumes by default, adds `--all` to prune `named` and `anonymous` volumes, and aligns filter docs/completion, volume prune e2e tests were also refactored to use `PodmanExitCleanly` with no functional change.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24597
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-4404

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
Breaking change: `podman volume prune` now defaults to pruning only anonymous unused volumes (Docker-compatible), adds `--all` for pruning all unused volumes.
```

comments.body: Good catch, let me check that. Could you give me a hint as to how you created those volumes? Or did you base them on a specific test scenario? I think I missed something.

> Good catch, let me check that. Could you give me a hint as to how you created those volumes? Or did you base them on a specific test scenario? I think I missed something.

```
# ./bin/podman system reset

# ./bin/podman volume create named1
named1
# ./bin/podman volume create named2
named2
# ./bin/podman volume create
<UNNAMED_STRING>

Followed by the `volume prune` and `volume ls` commands from earlier comment.
```

@lsm5 Very good catch. The `podman volume create (no name)` creates a volume with a random ID, but never sets `IsAnon`, so it stayed `Anonymous: false`. That is why it is not removed. Also, I have a bug in the listing of verbose prune.
@Honny1 I think the tests need to be adjusted.

> ```
> $ ./bin/podman volume prune
> WARNING! This will remove all volumes not used by at least one container. The following volumes will be removed:
> named1
> named2
> 5264366d0f02e3db807ccfe9dfffbd7ea687067e2b069abb15320a116db9dd3e
> Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
> named1
> named2
> 5264366d0f02e3db807ccfe9dfffbd7ea687067e2b069abb15320a116db9dd3e
> $ ./bin/podman volume ls
> DRIVER      VOLUME NAME
> $
> ```
> 
> LGTM

Lots of `crun: systemd failed to install eBPF device filter on cgroup ` errors in testing-farm rawhide job. May not be a blocker here but could bite us in bodhi.

LGTM
Four of the Fedora test failed with flakes.  I'll let @mheon or @Luap99 decide if they should complete or not before merge.
packit is not blocking and not related to this PR so good to merge, though these do look like real errors that need to be fixed I ping folks on matrix
